art_portfolio.philosophy: object permanence—reimagined. everyday icons. familiar forms. nostalgia in the making.
art_portfolio.artist_statement: I'm interested in the role objects play in our lives— the way they hold memory, carry meaning, and sometimes say more about us than we intend. These paintings are a kind of gamble. An attempt to mark what matters, or maybe what will. They're about connection, recognition, and the quiet joy of belonging to the same story— one object at a time.

content.heading: Fine Art Practice - justin-harmon.art
content.description: Exploring object permanence through contemporary painting, examining how everyday icons hold memory, carry meaning, and define cultural identity. Four distinct series investigate the role objects play in our lives: Bourbon Series (luxury spirits as cultural artifacts), Status Symbols Series (objects that define aspiration and identity), Common Ground Series (shared spaces and community), and Kid Collab Series (collaborative creativity). Artist statement: 'These paintings are a kind of gamble. An attempt to mark what matters, or maybe what will. They're about connection, recognition, and the quiet joy of belonging to the same story— one object at a time.'

header.tagline: Reimagining everyday experiences through design, technology, and art

recommended_profile_structure.about: Multi-disciplinary creative with 20+ years pioneering innovative solutions across industrial design, fine art, and technology. Known for founding the VSP Innovation Lab (revolutionizing eyewear prototyping), creating AI-powered platforms (8825 PCMS, Trustybits), and exploring object permanence through contemporary painting (justin-harmon.art). Philosophy: 'Breaking out of established patterns to look at things in a different way'—applying creative problem-solving across design, technology, and artistic expression.
